hyperedges.label: In-Process Background Jobs (survive a closed browser)

nodes.label: GitHub Sponsors Funding Config
nodes.rationale: Only alias handles are exposed publicly; platforms that would reveal a real name (e.g. PayPal.me) are deliberately excluded.

nodes.label: Multi-arch Docker Image (GHCR + optional Docker Hub)
nodes.rationale: GHCR is always published; Docker Hub is an optional mirror gated on DOCKERHUB_* secrets so Unraid and similar tools can auto-update from either registry.
nodes.norm_label: multi-arch docker image (ghcr + optional docker hub)

nodes.label: Server-side Device Proxy
nodes.rationale: The WiiM HTTP API has no auth and a self-signed cert, so the browser never contacts the device directly; all device traffic goes through authenticated Route Handlers.

nodes.label: Request Lifecycle (middleware → guard → Zod → device call → transport → parse)
nodes.norm_label: request lifecycle (middleware → guard → zod → device call → transport → parse)

nodes.label: SSRF Guard (resolve → LAN IP-check → pin)
nodes.rationale: DNS-resolve, verify private/LAN range, then pin the connection to the resolved IP — blocks DNS rebinding and keeps the proxy from reaching public or cloud-metadata hosts.

nodes.label: NAS/DLNA Library Module (src/lib/dlna)
nodes.rationale: The WiiM HTTP API cannot browse content, so the dashboard talks to the UPnP/DLNA media server directly; browse/art requests reuse the SSRF guard constrained to the configured media host.

nodes.label: AVTransport-driven NAS Playback
nodes.rationale: The httpapi setPlayerCmd:playlist push ignores the start index and resumes a stale playlist cursor (starts mid-album), so NAS playback drives the WiiM's own UPnP MediaRenderer (SetAVTransportURI + Play + SetNextAVTransportURI) instead — ordered, first-track start, real DIDL metadata.

nodes.label: Last.fm Background Scrobbler (poller)
nodes.rationale: Runs in the Node process (started from instrumentation.ts register()) so scrobbling works with no browser tab open; globalThis guards double-start; polls every 15 s per enabled device.

nodes.label: Love via Last.fm track.love/unlove
nodes.rationale: The WiiM HTTP API has no native favorite/like command — the WiiM app's heart calls each streaming service's cloud API which the server can't reach — so the dashboard's heart is wired through Last.fm instead.

nodes.label: Ignored-Scrobble Detection (scrobbles.@attr.ignored)
nodes.rationale: Last.fm returns HTTP 200 even when it silently drops a scrobble (e.g. artist 'Various Artists', its compilation anti-spam rule), so the client inspects scrobbles.@attr.ignored and logs the real outcome; ignored plays are not retried because a re-submit would be dropped again.

nodes.label: Server-side Sleep Timer Registry
nodes.rationale: Kept in the Node process so the scheduled pause fires even with no browser open; the snapshot exposes the expiry for a live countdown.

nodes.label: NAS Play-Queue Advancer (nas-queue.ts)
nodes.rationale: UPnP AVTransport only holds a current + one next URI, so an interval polls GetPositionInfo and loads the following track via SetNextAVTransportURI for ordered whole-album playback; also the source of truth for proxied cover art.

nodes.label: Capability Detection & Caching
nodes.rationale: detectCapabilities probes once on add/refresh and caches the result on the device row so the 3 s poll loop never re-probes.

nodes.label: 30 s Preset-List Cache
nodes.rationale: Shared between the poll and the 12 preset-art image requests to avoid an N+1 request storm on the embedded device.

nodes.label: Docker Standalone Deployment (non-root via gosu)
nodes.rationale: Entrypoint fixes data-dir ownership so bind-mounts (e.g. Unraid appdata) work, then drops to uid 1001 via gosu; the app never runs as root.

nodes.label: Typecheck + Build PR Gate
nodes.rationale: There is no test suite; `npm run typecheck && npm run build` is the mandatory gate before any PR.

nodes.label: Semantic Versioning & release.sh Flow
nodes.rationale: package.json is the single source of truth for the version, injected at build time via next.config.ts env.APP_VERSION, so nothing needs manual syncing.

nodes.label: Reverse Proxy / TLS Termination
nodes.rationale: The app serves plain HTTP; TLS is terminated at a reverse proxy. Because TRUST_PROXY=true honours X-Forwarded-For, only the proxy should reach the app port (bind to loopback when co-located).

nodes.label: Supported WiiM/LinkPlay Devices
nodes.rationale: Any device exposing httpapi.asp; non-networked accessories like the passive Vibelink Amp cannot be controlled.

nodes.label: Threat Model: Unauthenticated Device API
nodes.rationale: httpapi.asp has no authentication — anyone reaching the device IP fully controls it, including destructive commands — and serves a self-signed cert; therefore the device is treated as trusted-LAN-only and all access sits behind the app's own auth.

nodes.label: HMAC-hashed Server Sessions
nodes.rationale: Only the HMAC (keyed by AUTH_SECRET) of the random 32-byte session token is stored in SQLite, so a DB leak alone cannot forge sessions.

nodes.label: Per-IP + Global Login Rate Limiting
nodes.rationale: A global cap exists in addition to the per-IP limit so spoofing X-Forwarded-For to rotate the apparent IP cannot grant unlimited attempts; failed logins are timing-equalised against a dummy hash to avoid user enumeration.

nodes.label: Nonce-based CSP + Security Headers
nodes.rationale: strict-dynamic nonce CSP with no unsafe-inline scripts; HSTS and upgrade-insecure-requests are disabled when COOKIE_SECURE=false for plain-http LAN testing.

nodes.label: Destructive Commands Not Proxied
nodes.rationale: reboot, shutdown, factory reset and network config are intentionally not implemented/proxied for safety.

nodes.label: wiim-dashboard Compose Service
nodes.rationale: Uses a named volume (wiim-data) rather than a bind-mount because Docker initialises it with the image's /data ownership so the non-root uid 1001 can write SQLite; host bind-mounts would be root-owned on Linux.

nodes.label: Host Networking for SSDP Discovery
nodes.rationale: SSDP UDP multicast doesn't traverse Docker bridge networking; network_mode: host must replace the ports block to enable LAN auto-discovery.

nodes.label: httpapi.asp HTTPS Transport (self-signed + mTLS)
nodes.rationale: HTTPS on 443 with a self-signed cert (CN www.linkplay.com) requiring TLS verification off; some firmware (WiiM Ultra fw 5.x) requires the shared LinkPlay mTLS client cert embedded in linkplay-cert.ts.

nodes.label: getPlayerStatusEx (playback status)
nodes.rationale: Title/Artist/Album are hex-encoded UTF-8 and may contain HTML entities; left empty for Bluetooth, where metadata comes from getMetaInfo (AVRCP) instead.

nodes.label: getMetaInfo → metaData
nodes.rationale: No codec or vendor field exists anywhere in the WiiM API; the device reports decoded PCM bitDepth even for lossy streams, so bitRate is the reliable lossy-vs-lossless signal.

nodes.label: setPlayerCmd:playlist Index-Ignored Caveat
nodes.rationale: On WiiM Ultra fw 5.x the <index> is ignored: playback starts at a stale internal playlist cursor that cannot be reset by stop, playindex, switchindex, a .m3u extension, or unique content — which is why NAS/DLNA playback uses UPnP AVTransport instead.

nodes.label: Asymmetric Loop/Shuffle Read-vs-Write Tables
nodes.rationale: The read field `loop` and the write command `loopmode` use different tables on current firmware; the older python-linkplay/Home-Assistant enum mis-maps modes (e.g. sets Repeat-One when asked for Repeat-All).

nodes.label: plm_support Input Bitmask
nodes.rationale: Doesn't reliably flag every input (Ultra USB, HDMI ARC), so some sources are offered explicitly and the currently-playing source is always kept selectable.

nodes.label: mode → Streaming Service Mapping
nodes.rationale: There is no vendor field, so Connect/cast sessions map from dedicated mode codes and generic in-app streaming (mode 10/20) is guessed by sniffing the albumArtURI host.

nodes.label: Per-source LV2 EQ Commands (Eq10HP / EqNp)
nodes.rationale: Community-verified (WiiM Ultra fw5.2+); the EQ card self-disables if the device stops answering these commands (firmware kill-switch).

nodes.label: Presets (getPresetInfo / MCUKeyShortClick)
nodes.rationale: Preset slots are read-only over HTTP: they can be listed and played but not set or reordered from the API.

nodes.label: No Native Favorite/Like Command
nodes.rationale: Confirmed against the official PDF, python-linkplay and the community OpenAPI; the WiiM app's heart calls each streaming service's own cloud API which a server can't reach — so Love is implemented via Last.fm track.love.

nodes.label: Device Discovery (SSDP M-SEARCH + IP-range Scan)
nodes.rationale: SSDP needs UDP multicast (host networking); the /24 IP-range scan probing getStatusEx works in Docker bridge mode.
